perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Section 17.3, each party will pay all properly invoiced fees within [***] after its receipt of a proper invoice therefor. All payments hereunder will be invoiced in U.S. Dollars. All payments hereunder will be made by wire transfer to the account specified by each Party; provided that a Party shall provide at least [***] advance notice of any changes to its account. [***].
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Section 17.3, each party will pay all properly invoiced fees within thirty (30) days after its receipt of a proper invoice therefor. All payments hereunder will be invoiced in U.S. Dollars. All payments hereunder will be made by wire transfer to the account specified by each Party; provided that a Party shall provide at least sixty (60) days advance notice of any changes to its account. [***].
perturbation.explanation: The payment terms were modified to establish contradictory deadlines. The initial payment period was changed from an undisclosed number of days [***] to a concrete 'thirty (30) days'. Also, the advance notice for modifications to the account details was modified from undisclosed number of days [***] to a concrete 'sixty (60) days'. This change introduces an in-text contradiction with the following Section 25.2 where there is a termination for cause, however the 'cure' period remains undisclosed. Therefore, there is an uncertainty for cure period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement, immediately upon written notice to the other Party, if the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and such breach (a) is incapable of cure or (b) being capable of cure, remains uncured [***] after the breaching Party receives written notice from the non-breaching Party thereof.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a contradiction. The changed text specifies 'thirty (30) days' and 'sixty (60) days' for payment and account modification notice, respectively. The contradicted text discusses termination for a material breach but leaves the 'cure' period undefined [***]. Since the payment terms were modified and now have definite time periods, failure to pay within thirty days could be considered a material breach. However, the cure period is unknown due to the omission '[***]', which creates uncertainty and potential conflict if a dispute arises regarding the termination clause. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.

perturbation.original_text: Allscripts will provide Company with a non-binding [***] sales forecast for Allscripts' sales of eligibility and payment processing solutions during [***] within [***] of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: Allscripts will provide Company with a non-binding quarterly sales forecast for Allscripts' sales of eligibility and payment processing solutions during the subsequent quarter within fifteen (15) days of the end of each quarter.
perturbation.explanation: The sales forecast frequency and deadline have been altered, causing internal inconsistencies. The phrase '[***]' has been modified to 'quarterly,' and the deadline originally noted as '[***] within [***] of the Effective Date' is now 'within fifteen (15) days of the end of each quarter.' This adjustment leads to an in-text contradiction, particularly with Section 17.1(a)(i), where the timing of invoicing from Company to Allscripts is linked to the original, now-modified, sales forecast schedule.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (i) Invoicing for Eligibility and Benefits (E&B) Services. The Company will provide Allscripts with (1) an invoice for the fees set forth on Exhibit F for all E&B Transactions [***] and (2) a report with reasonably detailed supporting data for all such E&B Transactions by each Sublicensed Customer, excluding Legacy Customers, [***].
perturbation.justification: The modification of the sales forecast frequency and deadline creates a direct conflict with Section 17.1(a)(i). The original text implied a one-time forecast related to the Effective Date, while the changed text specifies a recurring quarterly forecast. Because the invoicing schedule in Section 17.1(a)(i) is tied to the original forecast schedule, the change makes it unclear when invoices should be sent, leading to an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines...creating in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced.'

perturbation.original_text: The initial term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and will continue in effect until five (5) year(s) from such date (the "Initial Term") unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 25.
perturbation.changed_text: The initial term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and will continue in effect until five (5) year(s) from such date (the "Initial Term") unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 25 or extended by mutual written agreement of both parties, not to exceed a total term of ten (10) years.
perturbation.explanation: A condition allowing extension of the initial term of the agreement has been added, creating a contradiction in terms. The original text specifies a fixed five-year initial term, but the revised text introduces the possibility of an extension via mutual agreement, up to a total of ten years. This change causes an in-text contradiction with Section 24.2, which details automatic renewal terms after the initial term expires. Now, it's unclear whether section 24.2 will be in effect, as it can be superseded by an agreement to extend to 10 years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless this Agreement is terminated pursuant to Section 25, this Agreement will automatically renew for additional successive [***] terms (each a "Renewal Term" and together with the Initial Term, the "Term") unless and until either Party provides written notice of non-renewal to the other Party at least [***] prior to the end of the then-current Term.
perturbation.justification: The addition of the extension clause in the changed text directly contradicts the automatic renewal terms outlined in the contradicted text (Section 24.2). The original text specifies a fixed five-year term followed by automatic renewals unless terminated, while the changed text allows for a mutual agreement to extend the initial term up to ten years, potentially bypassing the automatic renewal process described in Section 24.2. This creates uncertainty regarding the applicability of the renewal clause, as the initial term could now extend beyond five years without triggering automatic renewal, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.
